2026-04-21T16:21:01.9250285Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9250699Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9251044Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9251340Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9251570Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9251728Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-21T16:21:01.9252079Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9252308Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9252504Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9252773Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T16:21:01.9253025Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. 2026-04-21T16:24:15.6624985Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T16:24:01.062251655Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58407 by @m-redding\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58407 updated the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` README and CHANGELOG, adding entries for AZC0020 and AZC0101 and simplifying the README for this internal-only package. It also added individual documentation files `docs/AZC0020.md` and `docs/AZC0101.md`.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- `docs/AZC0012.md` does not exist — AZC0012 (Avoid single word type names) is the only implemented rule without its own dedicated documentation file. AZC0020 and AZC0101 both have individual `docs/\u003cRULE\u003e.md` files.\n- The README rules table lists all three rules (`AZC0012`, `AZC0020`, `AZC0101`) but does not hyperlink any of them to their individual documentation pages. AZC0020 and AZC0101 have standalone docs that should be linked; AZC0012 needs its own doc file created first.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Create `docs/AZC0012.md`\n\n**Path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0012.md`\n**Action:** Create the file\n\nUse `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0020.md` and `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0101.md` as templates. The content should include:\n\n```markdown\n# AZC0012: Avoid single word type names\n\n| Property | Value |\n|----------|-------|\n| **Severity** | Warning |\n| **Code fix** | No |\n\n## Cause\n\nPublic types (classes, interfaces, or structs) in Azure SDK namespaces (`Azure.*`, excluding `Azure.Core.*`) use single-word names, which are too generic and risk colliding with BCL types or types from other libraries.\n\n## Why this matters\n\nSingle-word type names such as `Format`, `Manager`, or `Options` are highly likely to conflict with types already defined in the .NET BCL or other libraries, leading to ambiguous references and confusing API surfaces.\n\n## How to fix\n\nUse descriptive multi-word names. Add a prefix or suffix that describes the purpose or domain of the type:\n\n```diff\nnamespace Azure.Data.Tables\n{\n-   public class Format { }\n+   public class TableDataFormat { }\n}\n```\n\n## When to suppress warnings\n\nSuppress only if the type name is genuinely well-known and intentionally mirrors a BCL type for compatibility reasons. Document the justification:\n\n```csharp\n#pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentional alias for well-known BCL type\npublic class Stream { }\n#pragma warning restore AZC0012\n```\n\n## Example of a violation\n\n```csharp\nnamespace Azure.Data.Tables\n{\n    public class Format { } // ❌ single-word name\n}\n```\n\n## Example of how to fix\n\n```csharp\nnamespace Azure.Data.Tables\n{\n    public class TableDataFormat { } // ✅ descriptive multi-word name\n}\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Only public types in Azure SDK namespaces (starting with `Azure.` except `Azure.Core.*`) are analyzed.\n- Interface names starting with `I` have the prefix ignored (e.g., `IClient` is treated as single-word).\n- Nested types are not analyzed.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Update the README rules table to link to individual docs\n\n**Path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md`\n**Action:** Edit — replace the existing `## Implemented Rules` table\n\nCurrent table:\n\n```markdown\n| Rule | Description | Fix |\n|------|-------------|-----|\n| **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | — |\n| **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |\n| **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |\n```\n\nReplace with:\n\n```markdown\n| Rule | Description | Fix |\n|------|-------------|-----|\n| [**AZC0012**](docs/AZC0012.md) | Avoid single word type names | — |\n| [**AZC0020**](docs/AZC0020.md) | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |\n| [**AZC0101**](docs/AZC0101.md) | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |\n```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` is intentionally simplified for an internal-only analyzer package and does not follow the standard customer-facing template. The current sections are appropriate. No further section changes are required.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`\n\n\u003e Note: `Update-Snippets.ps1` and `Export-API.ps1` are not required here — no public API surface changed and no snippet placeholders exist in this README.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","temporary_id":"aw_azc0012","title":"[Tools] Docs: Add missing AZC0012.md and link rules table in README"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-21T16:24:15.7072531Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7072921Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7073028Z **Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7073376Z **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7073684Z **Triggered by:** PR #58407 by @m-redding
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7073859Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7073944Z ## What Changed
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7074053Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7074791Z PR #58407 updated the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` README and CHANGELOG, adding entries for AZC0020 and AZC0101 and simplifying the README for this internal-only package. It also added individual documentation files `docs/AZC0020.md` and `docs/AZC0101.md`.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7075594Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7075764Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7075872Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7076630Z - `docs/AZC0012.md` does not exist — AZC0012 (Avoid single word type names) is the only implemented rule without its own dedicated documentation file. AZC0020 and AZC0101 both have individual `docs/<RULE>.md` files.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7078058Z - The README rules table lists all three rules (`AZC0012`, `AZC0020`, `AZC0101`) but does not hyperlink any of them to their individual documentation pages. AZC0020 and AZC0101 have standalone docs that should be linked; AZC0012 needs its own doc file created first.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7078877Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7078960Z <details>
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7079266Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7079565Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7079858Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7080247Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7080340Z ### Step 1: Create `docs/AZC0012.md`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7080521Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7080658Z **Path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0012.md`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7080961Z **Action:** Create the file
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7081111Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7081515Z Use `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0020.md` and `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/docs/AZC0101.md` as templates. The content should include:
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082005Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082080Z ```markdown
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082277Z # AZC0012: Avoid single word type names
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082444Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082534Z | Property | Value |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082740Z |----------|-------|
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7082946Z | **Severity** | Warning |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7083165Z | **Code fix** | No |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7083356Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7083429Z ## Cause
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7083536Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7084157Z Public types (classes, interfaces, or structs) in Azure SDK namespaces (`Azure.*`, excluding `Azure.Core.*`) use single-word names, which are too generic and risk colliding with BCL types or types from other libraries.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7084852Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7084929Z ## Why this matters
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7085054Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7085652Z Single-word type names such as `Format`, `Manager`, or `Options` are highly likely to conflict with types already defined in the .NET BCL or other libraries, leading to ambiguous references and confusing API surfaces.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7086333Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7086407Z ## How to fix
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7086519Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7086797Z Use descriptive multi-word names. Add a prefix or suffix that describes the purpose or domain of the type:
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7087166Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7087240Z ```diff
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7087425Z namespace Azure.Data.Tables
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7087645Z {
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7087826Z -   public class Format { }
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7088064Z +   public class TableDataFormat { }
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7088296Z }
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7088453Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7088550Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7088641Z ## When to suppress warnings
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7088792Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7089199Z Suppress only if the type name is genuinely well-known and intentionally mirrors a BCL type for compatibility reasons. Document the justification:
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7089700Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7089770Z ```csharp
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7090086Z #pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentional alias for well-known BCL type
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7090476Z public class Stream { }
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7090694Z #pragma warning restore AZC0012
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7090910Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7090998Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7091085Z ## Example of a violation
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7091222Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7091293Z ```csharp
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7091475Z namespace Azure.Data.Tables
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7091684Z {
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7091940Z     public class Format { } // ❌ single-word name
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7092207Z }
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7092362Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7092451Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7092622Z ## Example of how to fix
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7092756Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7092833Z ```csharp
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7093007Z namespace Azure.Data.Tables
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7093217Z {
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7093600Z     public class TableDataFormat { } // ✅ descriptive multi-word name
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7093921Z }
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7094076Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7094163Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7094242Z ## Notes
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7094333Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7094611Z - Only public types in Azure SDK namespaces (starting with `Azure.` except `Azure.Core.*`) are analyzed.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7095238Z - Interface names starting with `I` have the prefix ignored (e.g., `IClient` is treated as single-word).
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7095745Z - Nested types are not analyzed.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7095964Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7096058Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7096216Z ### Step 2: Update the README rules table to link to individual docs
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7096459Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7096583Z **Path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097004Z **Action:** Edit — replace the existing `## Implemented Rules` table
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097247Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097324Z Current table:
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097445Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097523Z ```markdown
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097720Z | Rule | Description | Fix |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7097954Z |------|-------------|-----|
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7098248Z | **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | — |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7098654Z | **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099060Z | **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099332Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099440Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099515Z Replace with:
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099631Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099702Z ```markdown
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7099891Z | Rule | Description | Fix |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7100116Z |------|-------------|-----|
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7100469Z | [**AZC0012**](docs/AZC0012.md) | Avoid single word type names | — |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7100966Z | [**AZC0020**](docs/AZC0020.md) | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7101472Z | [**AZC0101**](docs/AZC0101.md) | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ |
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7101798Z ```
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7101893Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7101978Z ### Step 3: Verify README structure
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7102145Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7102919Z The README at `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` is intentionally simplified for an internal-only analyzer package and does not follow the standard customer-facing template. The current sections are appropriate. No further section changes are required.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7103846Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7103924Z ### Step 4: Validate
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7104051Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7104211Z Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding:
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7104458Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7104572Z 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7104954Z 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7105331Z 3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7105524Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7106061Z > Note: `Update-Snippets.ps1` and `Export-API.ps1` are not required here — no public API surface changed and no snippet placeholders exist in this README.
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7106568Z 
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7106651Z </details>
2026-04-21T16:24:15.7106748Z
